mnemonics: LINES: Layer, Intercept, Navigate, Extend, Style
keyPoints: Use `geom_line()` to connect observations, ordered by the x-axis variable.
The `group` aesthetic is crucial for plotting multiple lines; without it, R may connect all points into a single line.
Customize line types, colors, and point markers with aesthetics like `linetype`, `color`, and `shape`.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to set `group` when plotting multiple lines for different categories, resulting in a single, tangled plot.
Using a categorical variable on the x-axis without ordering it, leading to an illogical and messy line path.
Overplotting by using a solid line for a very large dataset, making trends difficult to see.
